pattern: Merging only takes component-wise maxima, so only triplets that never exceed the target in any position can be safely used; the target is reachable when among those usable triplets each coordinate is hit exactly.
hints: Any triplet with a component larger than the target's would overshoot that component, so it can never be part of the merge.
Restrict attention to triplets whose every component is at most the corresponding target value.
From those usable triplets, the target is achievable only if some of them reach the target value in the first, second, and third position respectively.

edge_inputs.description: merge of two triplets reaches target
no triplet has the middle value so impossible
target reached by combining several usable triplets
a triplet overshoots one coordinate and is unusable
each coordinate satisfied by a different triplet
usable triplets exist but never reach the third coordinate

stress.description: random triplets and target, mostly infeasible by chance
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    triplets = [[rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(3)] for _ in range(n)]
    target = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(3)]
    return [triplets, target]

stress.description: construct feasible targets by inserting coordinate-achieving triplets
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    target = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(3)]
    triplets = []
    # add triplets that achieve each coordinate without overshooting
    triplets.append([target[0], rng.randint(1, target[1]), rng.randint(1, target[2])])
    triplets.append([rng.randint(1, target[0]), target[1], rng.randint(1, target[2])])
    triplets.append([rng.randint(1, target[0]), rng.randint(1, target[1]), target[2]])
    # add noise triplets that may overshoot
    for _ in range(rng.randint(0, 200)):
        triplets.append([rng.randint(1, 60) for _ in range(3)])
    rng.shuffle(triplets)
    return [triplets, target]
